title: astragalus caeruleus
intro: Astragalus caeruleus, also known as blue milk vetch, is a perennial legume plant native to the mountainous regions of Central Asia and China. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Astragalus caeruleus are said to include reducing fatigue, improving immune function, and preventing cardiovascular disease. In herbal preparations, Astragalus caeruleus can be found in the form of extracts, capsules, and teas, often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
